extension.description: Save files as shortcuts and run scripts. Single-click opens, double-click executes.
views.container.title: Saropa Workspace
views.pins.name: Shortcuts
views.recipes.name: Recipes
views.projectFiles.name: Project Files
viewsWelcome.pins.contents:
No shortcuts yet.
Add a file as a shortcut from the editor title menu or the Explorer right-click menu, then single-click to open it or double-click to run it.
[Add the active file as a shortcut](command:saropaWorkspace.pinActiveFile)
viewsWelcome.recipes.contents:
No recipes detected.
Recipes are auto-detected shortcuts derived from your project (open on GitHub, run scripts, Saropa Suite tools). They appear here when this workspace has files like .git/config, package.json, or a Makefile.
viewsWelcome.projectFiles.contents:
No recognized project files found in this workspace.
This view lists files like README, CHANGELOG, and package manifests with their last-modified time and version.

config.autoPins.patterns.description: Filenames or globs auto-added as shortcuts per project (removable). Removing an auto-shortcut keeps it removed.
config.doubleClickMs.description: Window in milliseconds for detecting a double-click on a shortcut. A second click within this window runs the file; a single click opens it.
config.previewMode.enabled.description: When on, single-clicking a shortcut file opens it in a transient Preview tab (italic title). Clicking another shortcut file reuses that tab, so clicking through several reference files does not flood the editor with tabs. Editing the file, or double-clicking a non-runnable shortcut, promotes the tab to permanent. Off by default: shortcuts open as permanent tabs.
config.defaultUseIntegratedTerminal.desc: Run shortcut scripts in the integrated terminal by default (so output is visible). When off, runs in the background and streams to an output channel.
config.terminalName.description: Name of the reused integrated terminal for shortcut scripts.
config.interpreterDefaults.description: Default command prefix per file extension, used when a shortcut has no explicit command set. An explicit per-shortcut command always wins.
config.suggestions.enabled.description: Offer to add a file you open often as a shortcut. Counts are kept on this machine only and never transmitted. Each file is offered at most once.
config.suggestions.openThreshold.descrip: How many times a file must be opened before Saropa Workspace offers to add it as a shortcut.
config.suggestPinnedTab.enabled.descript: Offer to add a file to your Saropa shortcuts when you have kept its editor tab pinned (right-click tab, Pin) past the threshold below. Tracked on this machine only and never transmitted. Each file is offered at most once; choosing Don't ask again suppresses it permanently (restore with Restore Pinned-Tab Suggestions).
config.suggestPinnedTab.afterHours.descr: How many hours a native editor tab must stay pinned before Saropa Workspace offers to add it to your shortcuts. A tab pinned before this was tracked starts counting from when the window opened.
config.projectFiles.enabled.description: Show the Project Files view, listing files like README, CHANGELOG, and package manifests with their last-modified time and declared version.
config.recipes.enabled.description: Show auto-detected recipes (URL, run, command, and macro shortcuts derived from project files such as .git/config, package.json, and Makefile) in a Recipes group. Removing a recipe keeps it removed; restore them with Restore Recipes.
config.branchAware.enabled.description: Switch the active shortcut set automatically to follow the current git branch. Link a branch to a set with 'Link Current Branch to Shortcut Set...'; on checkout, the linked set becomes active (and its optional on-switch shortcut runs). Off by default; outside a git repository it has no effect.
config.aiContext.enabled.description: Scan the configured chat folders for active Claude/AI conversations and surface the most recent ones as shortcuts in an Active AI Threads group. Removing a thread keeps it removed; restore them with Restore Recipes.
config.aiContext.claudeChatFolders.descr: Workspace-relative folders scanned for Claude/AI chat transcripts (.md or .json). The 10 most recently modified files per scan are offered as shortcuts: a Markdown file opens locally (or deep-links to claude.ai when it carries a chat URL), a JSON file deep-links to claude.ai by its id. Each folder is scanned at its top level only, never recursively.
config.telemetry.enabled.description: Keep a local, on-device record of shortcut runs (manual and scheduled) to power the Recent group and the recents in Run Shortcut. Stored on this machine only and never transmitted. Turn off to stop collecting; use Reset Run History to clear it.
config.projectFiles.files.description: Root-relative file names surfaced in the Project Files view. Each is shown only when it exists. Version is read from package.json, pubspec.yaml, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, and the top entry of CHANGELOG.md.
Sample your detected toolchain's processes on a timer while a workspace is open, appending a per-tool CPU/RAM/process-count row to reports/process-trend.csv and warning only when a tool crosses a RAM or helper-count ceiling. Off by default; the live monitor and snapshot work without it. Nothing is transmitted, and the heartbeat never ends a process.
config.processMonitor.heartbeat.interval: How often the toolchain heartbeat samples, in minutes (when enabled).
config.processMonitor.ramCeilingMB.descr: Per-tool working-set RAM ceiling, in megabytes. The heartbeat warns once when a tool (e.g. a leaked analysis server) exceeds it, and again only after it drops back under.
config.processMonitor.helperCountCeiling: Per-tool helper-process count ceiling. The heartbeat warns once when a tool (e.g. an editor helper swarm) exceeds it, and again only after it drops back under.
config.hygiene.mode.description: What the hygiene scan reports: empty (zero-byte files and zero-child folders), oversized (files and folders past the size ceilings), or both.
config.hygiene.fileMaxMB.description: Hygiene scan: a file larger than this many megabytes is reported as oversized.
config.hygiene.folderMaxMB.description: Hygiene scan: a folder whose recursive total exceeds this many megabytes is reported as oversized.
config.hygiene.fileMinMB.description: Hygiene scan: when greater than 0, a non-empty file smaller than this many megabytes is reported as under-size. 0 disables the floor check.
config.hygiene.respectGitignore.descript: Hygiene scan: read the project's top-level .gitignore and skip what it names (in addition to a built-in ignore set covering node_modules, .git, and build output). Turn off to scan ignored paths too.
config.hygiene.exclude.description: Hygiene scan: additional glob patterns to skip (matched against each entry's path relative to its scan root), e.g. "**/*.snap" or "coverage".
config.hygiene.bloat.folderCeilingMB.des: Workspace bloat scan: an immediate child directory whose recursive total exceeds this many megabytes is flagged as a crawl risk when it is not in files.watcherExclude. Default 1024 (1 GB).
config.hygiene.bloat.fileCountCeiling.de: Workspace bloat scan: an immediate child directory with more than this many files (recursive) is flagged as a crawl risk when it is not in files.watcherExclude. Default 50000.
config.hygiene.roots.description: Workspace bloat scan: extra parent folders (e.g. a src root holding sibling projects) to include in a cross-project morning sweep. Each child directory of a listed root is scanned as its own project. Empty (the default) scans only the open workspace folders. Findings in other projects are reported with their remediation; only the open workspace is guarded automatically.
config.sound.enabled.description: Play a short audio cue when a shortcut run starts or finishes. Off by default; the cue is additive to the on-screen toast and uses your OS's built-in system sounds (so it follows the OS volume and mute). The per-event toggles below choose which moments chime, and a shortcut's Configure Run can override this for that shortcut.
config.sound.onStart.description: When sound is enabled, play a cue when a run starts. Off by default (start cues can be chatty).
config.sound.onSuccess.description: When sound is enabled, play a distinct success cue when a run finishes successfully. Background and report runs report an outcome; terminal and external-window runs do not, so they cue only on start.
config.sound.onFailure.description: When sound is enabled, play a distinct failure cue when a run exits with an error.
